docs: https://www.braze.com/docs/api/home/ ; endpoint set cross-checked against the community-maintained OpenAPI spec (https://github.com/braze-community/braze-specification/blob/master/openapi/spec.json, 82 documented paths)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every documented Braze REST endpoint (export, messaging, user data, subscription groups, catalogs, templates/content blocks, email lists, SMS, feed/Content Cards, KPIs, sessions, purchases, preference center, SCIM) is enumerated below. 21 read streams now covered: campaigns/canvases/segments (list), campaign_details/canvas_details/segment_details/canvas_data_summary (per-id fan_out over each list stream's own ids), catalogs, content_blocks, email_templates, feed_cards, email_hard_bounces, email_unsubscribes, sms_invalid_phone_numbers, kpi_dau/kpi_mau/kpi_new_users/kpi_uninstalls, sessions, preference_centers, scheduled_broadcasts. 29 write actions covered: user track/identify/delete/merge/alias-new/alias-update/external-id-remove/external-id-rename, subscription status set v2 (batch), catalog create/delete + catalog item create/update/delete (batch and single), content block create/update, email template create/update, email blocklist/bounce-remove/spam-remove/status-set, sms invalid-number remove, messages send + campaign/canvas trigger-send, preference center create/update. Two ENGINE_GAP exclusions carry over/recur: the `events` stream (unchanged from wave2) and the newly-identified `/purchases/product_list` stream — both return a bare array of NAME STRINGS, not objects, which the engine's declarative array extraction (connsdk.RecordsAt) silently drops; see docs.md Known limits. Excluded as genuinely out of scope: async broadcast/trigger SCHEDULING (create/update/delete of a future send — a fire-and-forget async job with its own lifecycle, not a plain data mutation this dialect's synchronous write model fits), live activity updates (a real-time push protocol extension, not a REST CRUD action), SCIM user provisioning (a distinct, separately-authenticated identity-management protocol, not Braze's own data model), transactional campaign send (a v1-prefixed, separately-entitled product surface), analytics data_series/data_summary breakdown matrices beyond the single-object summary already covered, and destructive/duplicate/non-data endpoints per the notes on each entry below.

excluded.reason: per-campaign analytics time series requiring a length/ending_at window coupling for a single campaign id; part of the analytics-breakdown surface excluded this pass for breadth-vs-cost triage, distinct from the already-covered list/details metadata streams
per-Canvas step/variant-breakdown analytics time series; part of the analytics-breakdown surface excluded this pass for breadth-vs-cost triage

catalog ITEM listing requires an already-known catalog_name (available via the catalogs stream) and a separate per-catalog request/pagination sequence with no engine fan_out wired to this bundle's catalogs stream this pass; the catalogs stream itself already syncs every catalog's name/description/fields/item-count metadata

bulk full-replace of catalog items; the same item-array upsert shape create_catalog_items (POST) already covers, using PUT semantics this dialect's single body_type/method-per-action model does not need a second action to express
bulk multi-item delete-by-id-array over the same catalog the single-item delete_catalog_item action already covers per-record
single catalog item detail; requires an item_id already in hand with no list-discovery endpoint feeding it (catalog items list is excluded above for the same reason), not a distinct syncable resource
single-item create-by-id variant of the batch create_catalog_items action, same underlying mutation shape
single-item full-replace variant of the batch update_catalog_items action, same underlying mutation shape
single content block detail; identical record shape to the content_blocks list stream's per-item object plus its own content body, already covered by that stream for sync purposes
legacy alias of the identical POST /email/blocklist mutation (Braze's own docs deprecate the blacklist wording in favor of blocklist); create_email_blocklist already covers this mutation shape
per-custom-event-name analytics time series requiring an event name already in hand; part of the analytics-breakdown surface excluded this pass for breadth-vs-cost triage

engine's declarative array extraction (connsdk.RecordsAt) only yields JSON-object array elements; Braze's events/list returns an array of bare event-name STRINGS, which RecordsAt silently drops rather than wrapping into a record the way legacy's decodeRecords does; see docs.md Known limits (ENGINE_GAP, unchanged from wave2)
per-Content-Card analytics time series requiring a card_id already in hand; part of the analytics-breakdown surface excluded this pass for breadth-vs-cost triage
single Content Card detail; identical record shape to the feed_cards list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
real-time Live Activity push-state update, a narrow iOS-specific messaging extension distinct from Braze's general campaign/data model; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage

creates an asynchronous scheduled-send job with its own lifecycle (a schedule_id to later update/cancel), not a plain synchronous data mutation this dialect's fire-once write model fits; messages/send (immediate send) already covers the core messaging mutation shape
cancels a job created by the excluded messages/schedule/create; no scheduled job is ever created by this bundle to cancel
mutates a job created by the excluded messages/schedule/create; no scheduled job is ever created by this bundle to update
single preference center detail; identical record shape to the preference_centers list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
generates a one-time signed preference-center URL for a specific user, not a syncable object or a data mutation

same ENGINE_GAP class as /events/list: the response's products array is a bare list of NAME STRINGS, not objects (`{"products": ["product_name", ...]}`), which the engine's declarative array extraction (connsdk.RecordsAt) silently drops rather than wrapping into a one-field record; see docs.md Known limits
per-product purchase-quantity analytics time series requiring a product name already in hand; part of the analytics-breakdown surface excluded this pass for breadth-vs-cost triage
per-product revenue analytics time series requiring a product name already in hand; part of the analytics-breakdown surface excluded this pass for breadth-vs-cost triage

SCIM 2.0 identity list is a distinct, separately-authenticated (bearer SCIM token, not the standard Braze API key) protocol for managing Braze DASHBOARD USER accounts, not Braze's own customer/campaign data model; out of scope for this data connector

SCIM 2.0 identity provisioning is a distinct, separately-authenticated (bearer SCIM token, not the standard Braze API key) protocol for managing Braze DASHBOARD USER accounts, not Braze's own customer/campaign data model; out of scope for this data connector
SCIM 2.0 identity read; same separately-authenticated dashboard-user-provisioning protocol as the SCIM write endpoints, out of scope for this data connector
SCIM 2.0 identity update; same separately-authenticated dashboard-user-provisioning protocol, out of scope for this data connector
SCIM 2.0 identity deprovisioning; same separately-authenticated dashboard-user-provisioning protocol, out of scope for this data connector
per-segment size-over-time analytics time series requiring a segment id already in hand; part of the analytics-breakdown surface excluded this pass for breadth-vs-cost triage

per-send-id analytics time series requiring a send_id already in hand (send ids are generated at send time, not enumerable via any list endpoint); part of the analytics-breakdown surface excluded this pass for breadth-vs-cost triage
pre-generates a send_id label for a future campaign trigger send; a naming/bookkeeping helper, not itself a data mutation on any Braze object
single-user subscription-group status lookup keyed by an external_id/email already in hand, with no list/enumeration variant; not a syncable collection stream

single-user, single-subscription-group variant superseded by the v2 batch endpoint (POST /v2/subscription/status/set), which set_subscription_status_v2 covers with the identical effect for any input the v1 shape accepts plus multi-group batching
single-user subscription status lookup by external_id/email already in hand, with no list/enumeration variant; not a syncable collection stream
single email template detail; identical record shape to the email_templates list stream's per-item object plus its own body content, already covered by that stream for sync purposes

Braze Transactional Messaging is a separately-entitled product surface (its own v1-prefixed API family with its own onboarding) layered on top of standard campaigns, not part of the base messaging surface send_message/trigger writes already cover
asynchronous bulk export job kickoff (delivered later to cloud storage), not a synchronous read/write record operation; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
asynchronous bulk export job kickoff for a caller-supplied id list, not a synchronous read/write record operation; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
asynchronous bulk export job kickoff for an entire segment's membership, not a synchronous read/write record operation; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage

creates an asynchronous scheduled API-triggered-campaign job with its own lifecycle, not a plain synchronous data mutation this dialect's fire-once write model fits; trigger_campaign_send (immediate trigger) already covers the core API-triggered-campaign mutation shape
cancels a job created by the excluded campaigns/trigger/schedule/create; no scheduled job is ever created by this bundle to cancel
mutates a job created by the excluded campaigns/trigger/schedule/create; no scheduled job is ever created by this bundle to update

creates an asynchronous scheduled API-triggered-Canvas job with its own lifecycle, not a plain synchronous data mutation this dialect's fire-once write model fits; trigger_canvas_send (immediate trigger) already covers the core API-triggered-Canvas mutation shape
cancels a job created by the excluded canvas/trigger/schedule/create; no scheduled job is ever created by this bundle to cancel
mutates a job created by the excluded canvas/trigger/schedule/create; no scheduled job is ever created by this bundle to update
